name: Tera Valley Loop
name_original: Camarzana de Tera a Calzadilla de Tera a Villanueva de las Peras — circular desde Santa Croya de Tera
description: This moderate 22.1-mile (35.5 km) mountain biking route from Santa Croya de Tera explores the varied landscapes of the Tera region.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Santa Croya De Tera offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by the Tera River and surrounding hills. The region provides a network of dirt roads and trails suitable for exploration. These mountain bike trails in Santa Croya De Tera traverse varied terrain, from riverside paths to more elevated areas, offering different challenges for cyclists. Best mountain bike trails around Santa Croya De Tera The most popular mountain bike trail is Castro de las Labradas Loop from Brime de Urz, a 24.0 miles (38.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 28 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Camino Sanabrés and Agavanzal Reservoir Loop, a moderate 22.2 miles (35.8 km) path. This route combines historical elements of the Camino Sanabrés with views of the Agavanzal Reservoir. Local mountain bikers also love the Santa María de Valverde - Camino Sanabrés Loop, a 16.3 miles (26.3 km) trail leading through the Tera River valley, often completed in about 2 hours 13 minutes. Mountain biking around Santa Croya De Tera is defined by the Tera River, surrounding hills, and a mix of dirt roads and established trails. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Santa Croya De Tera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 1 review. More than 20 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Santa Croya De Tera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Santa Croya De Tera?
A: There are 10 mountain bike trails around Santa Croya De Tera, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Santa Croya De Tera?
A: The region offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by the Tera River and surrounding hills. You can expect a mix of dirt roads and established trails, ranging from riverside paths to more elevated areas. This provides varied terrain suitable for different challenges.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Santa Croya De Tera?
A: Yes, there are 2 easy mountain bike routes available, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. The overall network includes options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I see while mountain biking in the area?
A: You can incorporate historical and cultural attractions into your rides. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5509742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa Marta de Tera&lt;/a&gt;, located on the Camino Sanabrés, is a significant landmark. The ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5453127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granucillo Castle&lt;/a&gt; also offer a glimpse into the region's history. The area near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5473859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camarzana de Tera&lt;/a&gt; also has Roman history, including a beautiful Roman villa.

Q: Are there any long or challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, the area offers a difficult route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40010177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintos Bridge – Church of Santa Marta de Tera loop from Urrieta los Aladiernos&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 84.9 km with significant elevation gain. There are also 7 moderate routes for those looking for a good workout.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Santa Croya De Tera?
A: The region's diverse landscapes are enjoyable throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. The Tera River environment provides pleasant conditions, and the unique illumination of the Church of Santa Marta de Tera during equinoxes can be a special experience.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Santa Croya De Tera?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1233718353/castro-de-las-labradas-loop-from-brime-de-urz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de las Labradas Loop from Brime de Urz&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1233510846/camino-sanabres-and-agavanzal-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Sanabrés and Agavanzal Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options, allowing you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Santa Croya De Tera?
A: The routes in Santa Croya De Tera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. More than 20 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse natural landscapes and the mix of riverside paths and elevated areas.

Q: Are there places to rest or have a picnic along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region offers convenient spots for breaks. You can find shady &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3858806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic Areas by the River&lt;/a&gt;, which are perfect for enjoying the natural surroundings during your rides.

Q: Can I combine mountain biking with historical exploration of the Camino Sanabrés?
A: Absolutely. Several routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1233510846/camino-sanabres-and-agavanzal-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Sanabrés and Agavanzal Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1238655309/santa-maria-de-valverde-camino-sanabres-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Valverde - Camino Sanabrés Loop&lt;/a&gt;, incorporate sections of the historic Camino Sanabrés, allowing you to experience both the natural beauty and the cultural heritage of the area.

Q: Are there any accommodations or hostels near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, for those looking to stay overnight, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6309776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Anita Tourist Hostel&lt;/a&gt; is an option in the area, providing a convenient base for exploring the trails.
tips: A small town next to the Tera river valley, fertile lands that were occupied from the Celts. There is a beautiful Roman villa in the village with a mosaic floor.
This is a great trail for mountain biking and even gravel biking. It has some ups and downs, but overall it's fast and fun.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route, officially named Camarzana de Tera a Calzadilla de Tera a Villanueva de las Peras — circular desde Santa Croya de Tera, is rated as moderate. While specific detailed information about this particular route's terrain is not readily available, moderate mountain biking trails typically involve some climbs, descents, and varied surfaces that require a reasonable level of fitness and basic biking skills.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, as indicated by its name. You will start and finish in the same general area, making it convenient for planning your ride.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is described as circular from Santa Croya de Tera, suggesting this is the primary starting point. While specific parking facilities for this exact route are not documented, you would typically look for public parking options within or near Santa Croya de Tera. Given the rural nature of the area, street parking might be available, but it's always best to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: Specific details for this exact route are limited. However, mountain biking in the Castile and León region can vary significantly. You might encounter anything from compacted gravel surfaces, similar to those found on "Vías Verdes" (greenways), to more challenging sections with rocky terrain or dirt paths. The route includes a highlight called Dirt Path from Melgar de Tera to Villanueva de las Peras, suggesting unpaved sections.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: There are no specific restrictions mentioned for dogs on this particular route. Generally, dogs are welcome on many trails in Spain, especially in rural areas, but it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near livestock, and to clean up after them. Please respect local regulations and other trail users.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride in this region generally depends on weather conditions. Spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) often offer pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather, making them ideal. Summers can be very hot, especially in inland Spain, while winters can be cold and potentially wet. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this mountain biking route. It appears to be a public access route through the local landscape.

faqs.question: What points of interest or landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several villages and natural areas. Notable highlights include the settlement of Camarzana de Tera and a specific section known as the Dirt Path from Melgar de Tera to Villanueva de las Peras. These offer glimpses into the local culture and natural environment of the Tera region.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will encounter parts of the Camino Sanabrés (East), the Mtb Camino Sanabrés (Este) / Mtb Camiño Xabrés (Leste), and the Camino Sanabrés. A small portion also follows Calle Mediodía.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to get refreshments along the route or nearby?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several villages, including Camarzana de Tera, Calzadilla de Tera, Villanueva de las Peras, and Santa Croya de Tera. These settlements are likely to offer local cafes, bars, or small shops where you can find refreshments. It's a good idea to carry some snacks and water, especially if riding outside of typical village opening hours.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: For a moderate mountain bike route of this distance (approximately 35.5 km), you should pack essentials such as sufficient water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing for the weather conditions. A helmet is essential, and sunglasses and sunscreen are recommended. Given the potential for varied terrain, consider a bike suitable for mixed surfaces.
